architecture.summary: MedAdapter is a post-hoc test-time adapter that ranks candidate solutions generated by an LLM without modifying the LLM itself. A small BERT-sized adapter network is trained to score or rank multiple solution candidates produced by the frozen LLM, selecting the best one at inference time. The adapter receives representations of candidate solutions and outputs ranking scores that guide solution selection.
trained_components: BERT-sized adapter network for ranking/scoring solutions

biblio.title: MedAdapter: Efficient Test-Time Adaptation of Large Language Models Towards Medical Reasoning

claims.dataset: four biomedical tasks across eight datasets
claims.statement: MedAdapter achieves average performance improvements of 18.24% on white-box LLMs

claims.dataset: four biomedical tasks across eight datasets
claims.statement: MedAdapter achieves average performance improvements of 10.96% on black-box LLMs
MedAdapter effectively adapts both white-box and black-box LLMs in biomedical reasoning without requiring extensive computational resources or sharing data with third parties
MedAdapter yields enhanced performance when combined with train-time adaptation, highlighting a flexible and complementary solution to existing adaptation methods
MedAdapter adapts the original model by fine-tuning only a small BERT-sized adapter to rank candidate solutions generated by LLMs instead of fine-tuning the entire LLM
